# Melker Engine

> A terminal app document format with built-in UI, permissions, and runtime. Melker treats terminal apps as documents you can inspect before you run. A .melker file contains markup, styles, a permission policy, and a script — all in one readable file.

- [Home](https://melker.sh/)
- [How it works](https://melker.sh/how-it-works.html)
- [Tutorial](https://melker.sh/tutorial.html)
- [AI Assistant](https://melker.sh/ai.html)
- [Manifesto](https://melker.sh/reference/MANIFESTO.md)

## Overview

- [CLAUDE.md (project guide)](https://melker.sh/reference/CLAUDE.md)
- [README](https://melker.sh/reference/README.md)
- [Manifesto](https://melker.sh/reference/MANIFESTO.md)
- [FAQ](https://melker.sh/reference/FAQ.md)
